twitch.description: You can close this window now

kick.description: You can close this window now

youtube.description: You can close this window now

welcome.title: Desktop Bitrate Monitor
welcome.description: Initializing data sources... the dashboard will appear automatically when the startup checks finish.

dashboard.description: Monitor your stream bitrate and performance in real-time.

feedChart.uptimeToolTip: Stream Uptime: Only since the current feed has been started
feedChart.totalUptimeToolTip: Total Uptime: sum of all feed uptimes since first connection detected since app start
feedChart.description: Real-time bitrate feed from your server.

activePlatform.noData: No broadcaster connected for the selected platform

users.description: Manage admin and mod users. These users do not have to be actual Twitch admins or moderators, these are just roles within the app that can be assigned to any Twitch user. ALL your twitch moderators are automaticaly asigned as moderators in the app. Admins have access to all commands, mods have access to all commands except those that are restricted to admins only.
users.noAccountRegistered: Please set a broadcaster acccount in the accounts tab to manage admins and mods
users.error5: Unable to validate user right now
users.inputAdornmentAdmin: Click or press Enter to add admin
users.inputAdornmentMod: Click or press Enter to add mod

admins.description: Admins are able to use all admin and lower role commands

mods.description: Moderators are able to use all mod and lower role commands

accounts.description: Log in with your broadcaster and bot accounts here. You can choose which account posts messages in the Twitch chat. The fallback is the broadcaster account. The application automatically listens to the broadcaster's Twitch account when Twitch is the active platform.
accounts.loggedOutBroadcaster: Broadcaster account disconnected successfully, tokens are revoked, data deleted from the app
accounts.loggedOutChatbot: Chatbot account disconnected successfully, tokens are revoked, data deleted from the app
accounts.enabledChatbot: Chatbot account now in usage for messages
accounts.disabledChatbot: Broadcaster account now in usage for messages

broadcaster.description: Log in with your broadcaster account here

chatbot.description: Log in with your chatbot account here
chatbot.hint: Use the chatbot account to post messages in the chat

users.description: Manage admin and mod users. These users do not have to be actual Kick admins or moderators, these are just roles within the app that can be assigned to any Kick user. ALL your Kick moderators are automaticaly asigned as moderators in the app. Admins have access to all commands, mods have access to all commands except those that are restricted to admins only.
users.noAccountRegistered: Please set a broadcaster acccount in the accounts tab to manage admins and mods
users.error5: Unable to validate user right now
users.inputAdornmentAdmin: Click or press Enter to add admin
users.inputAdornmentMod: Click or press Enter to add mod

admins.description: Admins are able to use all admin and lower role commands

mods.description: Moderators are able to use all mod and lower role commands

accounts.description: Log in with your broadcaster and bot accounts here. You can choose which account posts messages in the Kick chat. The fallback is the broadcaster account. The application automatically listens to the broadcaster's Kick account when Kick is the active platform.
accounts.loggedOutBroadcaster: Broadcaster account disconnected successfully, tokens are revoked, data deleted from the app
accounts.loggedOutChatbot: Chatbot account disconnected successfully, tokens are revoked, data deleted from the app
accounts.enabledChatbot: Chatbot account now in usage for messages
accounts.disabledChatbot: Broadcaster account now in usage for messages

broadcaster.description: Log in with your broadcaster account here

chatbot.description: Log in with your chatbot account here
chatbot.hint: Use the chatbot account to post messages in the chat

commands.description: Configure chat commands that can be used to trigger actions in the app.
commands.restrictedHint: If restricted, only the broadcaster and admins can switch away from the privacy scene

aliasBox.label: Add Alias
aliasBox.hint: Prefix must be included in the alias, e.g., !
aliasBox.inputAdornment: Click or press Enter to add

startStream.label: Start Stream
startStream.description: Starts the stream in the software to the platform

stopStream.label: Stop Stream
stopStream.description: Stops the stream in the software to the platform

addAdmin.label: Add Admin
addAdmin.description: Adds a new admin, e.g., !addadmin username

addMod.label: Add Moderator
addMod.description: Adds a new moderator, e.g., !addmod username. The user does NOT need to be a Twitch moderator!

switchScene.label: Switch Scene
switchScene.description: Switches to any scene in the streaming software. Restrictive rules remain in place, the scene must be entered exactly, e.g., !ss scene gaming. Case sensitivity is not observed.

setTrigger.label: Set Trigger
setTrigger.description: Sets the new value for switching to the low-bitrate scene, only numeric values are accepted

setRTrigger.label: Set Recovery Trigger
setRTrigger.description: Sets the new value for switching back to the live scene, only numeric values are accepted

bitrate.label: Get Bitrate
bitrate.description: Returns the current bitrate and speed

messages.description: Configure messages that are posted in the chat when actions are triggered.
messages.subtitle: Message to post in the chat
messages.save: Click or press Enter to save

success.label: Raid Success Message
success.hint: ${channel} will be replaced with the name of the raided channel.

error.label: Raid Error Message
error.hint: ${channel} will be replaced with the name of the raided channel.

success.label: Bitrate Success Message
success.hint: ${bitrate} will be replaced with the current bitrate and ${speed} with the current speed.

success.label: Switch Scene Success Message
success.hint: ${scene} will be replaced with the name of the switched scene.

error.label: Switch Scene Error Message
error.hint: ${scene} will be replaced with the name of the switched scene.

error.label: Set Trigger Error Message
error.hint: ${trigger} will be replaced with the set bitrate trigger in kbps.

success.label: Set Recovery Trigger Success Message
success.hint: ${trigger} will be replaced with the set bitrate recovery trigger in kbps.

error.label: Set Recovery Trigger Error Message
error.hint: ${trigger} will be replaced with the set bitrate recovery trigger in kbps.

alreadyAdmin.label: Admin Already Exists Message
alreadyAdmin.hint: ${user} will be replaced with the name of the admin being added.

error.label: Add Admin Error Message
error.hint: ${user} will be replaced with the name of the admin being added.

success.label: Remove Admin Success Message

success.label: Add Mod Success Message

alreadyMod.label: Mod Already Exists Message

success.label: Remove Mod Success Message

notFound.label: Remove Mod Not Found Message

server.description: Edit the settings for the streaming server to listen to the stats
server.error2: Stats URL must start with http:// or https://
server.inputAdornment: Click or press Enter to save

srtLiveServer.description: Configure the settings for the SrtLiveServer connection

nameBox.label: Server Name
nameBox.hint: The name of the server instance, e.g., SrtLiveServer

openIrl.description: Configure the settings for the OpenIRL connection

nameBox.label: Server Name
nameBox.hint: The name of the server instance, e.g., OpenIRL

belabox.description: Configure the settings for the Belabox connection

nameBox.label: Server Name
nameBox.hint: The name of the server instance, e.g., Belabox

software.description: Choose and configure your streaming software settings here
software.inputAdornment: Click or press Enter to save
software.reconnectErrorMessage: Failed to reconnect to streaming software: {{error}}

hostBox.label: OBS Websocket Host
hostBox.hint: Host should be localhost or public IP address

passwordBox.label: OBS Websocket Password
passwordBox.hint: The password for the OBS Websocket connection

hostBox.label: Streamlabs OBS Websocket Host
hostBox.hint: Host should be localhost or public IP address

passwordBox.label: API-Token
passwordBox.hint: The API-Token for the Streamlabs OBS Websocket connection

hostBox.label: MELD Studio Websocket Host
hostBox.hint: Host should be localhost or public IP address

passwordBox.label: MELD Studio Websocket Password
passwordBox.hint: The password for the MELD Studio Websocket connection

switcher.inputAdornment: Click or press Enter to save

states.switchToStartSceneOnStreamStart: Switch to Start Scene on Stream Start

scenes.description: Setup the broadcasting software scenes here. The scene names must match exactly with the scenes in your broadcasting software (case insensitive)
scenes.error2: {{scene}} cannot be the same as Privacy Scene
scenes.error3: Privacy Scene has to be unique

triggers.description: Setup the bitrate triggers and timeout values for switching scenes
triggers.error3: {{value}} must be lower than Reverse to Live Trigger
triggers.error4: {{value}} must be greater than Trigger to Low
triggers.error6: {{value}} must be greater than Offline Trigger
triggers.error5: {{value}} must be lower than both Trigger to Low and Reverse to Live Trigger

trigger.label: Bitrate Trigger to Low Scene (kbps)
trigger.hint: The bitrate threshold in kbps for switching to the low-bitrate scene

rTrigger.label: Bitrate Recovery Trigger to Live Scene (kbps)
rTrigger.hint: The bitrate threshold in kbps for switching back to the live scene

offlineTrigger.label: Bitrate Trigger to Offline Scene (kbps)
offlineTrigger.hint: The bitrate threshold in kbps for switching to the offline scene

switchToLiveTimeout.label: Switch to Live Timeout (s)
switchToLiveTimeout.hint: The time in seconds that the bitrate must be above the recovery trigger to switch back to the live scene. 0 forces an instant switch back to the live scene

switchToOfflineTimeout.label: Switch to Offline Timeout (s)
switchToOfflineTimeout.hint: The time in seconds that the bitrate must be below the offline trigger to switch to the offline scene

switchToLowTimeout.label: Switch to Low-Bitrate Timeout (s)
switchToLowTimeout.hint: The time in seconds that the bitrate must be below the low trigger to switch to the low-bitrate scene

settings.description: Set the logging paths and states
settings.sessionCardDescription: Set the logging settings for session logs
settings.actionsCardDescription: Set the logging settings for actions logs

validation.fileSizeInteger: File size must be an integer
validation.fileSizeRange: File size must be between 0 and 10.

style.description: Configure the appearance of the application here

updateApp.description: Download starts immediately. Once the download is complete, the update will be installed.

noUpdateAvailable.description: You are already using the latest version of the app.

overlayEditor.description: Settings for the bitrate stats overlay

global.error: Something went wrong! Please try again.

stopStream.success: Stream has stopped. Thanks for watching!

raid.success: Follow us to twitch.tv/${channel} for more content!
raid.hint: ${channel} will be replaced with the raided channel name.

bitrate.hint: ${bitrate} will be replaced with the current bitrate and ${speed} with the current speed.

switchScene.hint: ${scene} will be replaced with the name of the switched scene.

setTrigger.error: Failed to set bitrate trigger to ${trigger} kbps!
setTrigger.hint: ${trigger} will be replaced with the set bitrate trigger.

setRTrigger.success: Bitrate recovery trigger set to ${rtrigger} kbps!
setRTrigger.error: Failed to set bitrate recovery trigger to ${rtrigger} kbps!
setRTrigger.hint: ${rtrigger} will be replaced with the set bitrate recovery trigger.

addAdmin.error: Failed to add user ${user} as an admin.
addAdmin.hint: ${user} will be replaced with the admin to add username.

removeAdmin.success: User ${user} has been removed from admins.
removeAdmin.error: Failed to remove user ${user} from admins.
removeAdmin.hint: ${user} will be replaced with the admin to remove username.

addMod.success: User ${user} has been added as a moderator.
addMod.error: Failed to add user ${user} as a moderator.
addMod.hint: ${user} will be replaced with the moderator to add username.

removeMod.success: User ${user} has been removed from moderators.
removeMod.error: Failed to remove user ${user} from moderators.
